title: The Philosopher's Trap
editorialSummary: Philosophical terms that each belong to exactly one tradition but share surface vocabulary across traditions. False trail 1: Dasein, Being-in-the-World, Thrownness, and Angst all read as Existentialist, but Heidegger belongs to Phenomenology — Intentionality (Husserl/Brentano) anchors the Phenomenology group instead of Angst, which is used by Kierkegaard (Existentialism) but here is absent. False trail 2: Categorical Imperative, Autonomy, and Duty all map to Kantian Deontology, but Autonomy is also central to Libertarian political philosophy — here it stays in the Kantian group because Kingdom of Ends is the confirming anchor. False trail 3: Eudaimonia, Telos, and Virtue all feel Aristotelian — Telos appears in both metaphysics and ethics but is placed in Virtue Ethics here as its primary ethical application. False trail 4: Dialectic is used by both Hegel and Marx — placed in Hegelian Idealism because Geist and Aufhebung are unmistakably Hegel.

meta.gptRationale: This is highly abstract and terminology-dense, with several schools of philosophy that many players will only partially recognize.
